title: Lisianthius corymbosus
taxonomy: Lisianthius corymbosus is classified as a species within the genus Lisianthus and the family Polemoniaceae. This classification places it within the order Polemoniales. Further, Lisianthius corymbosus is categorized as a dicotyledonous flowering plant. The classification of Lisianthius corymbosus is also reflective of its unique characteristics and botanical features. Its classification serves as an important tool for botanists in identifying and studying this plant species.

The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The roots and leaves of Lisianthius corymbosus are used to prepare infusions and decoctions that are believed to have antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of the plant's medicinal uses.
